details.text: Derivative: الْمُدَّثِّرُ (Al-Muddaththir) means to cover up as opposed to الْمُزَّمِّل (Al-Muzzammil) which means to wrap up.
Derivative: وَحِيدًا means alone and can imply being an only child or lonely.

- ذَرْنِي accompanied or followed by وَ means: “Leave me (ذَرْنِي) to deal with (وَ) such and such a person or matter”. It is used to express the meaning of: “Don’t concern yourself with this, I will deal with it all by myself”.
Derivative: جَعَلْتُ from جَعَلَ means to make. When followed by ل (for/to) it means to provide, furnish and make something available to someone.
Derivative: مَّمْدُودًا means to keep extending. Here it refers to wealth that continues to expand and grow.
Derivative: شُهُودًا is the plural of شَاهِد (present) which is in the حال (Haal) form meaning that they present and around with him in life.
Derivative: مَهَّدتُّ from مَهَّدَ – تَمْهِيدًا means to spread out, smoothen and facilitate i.e., to pave the way and make things easy.
Derivative: كَلَّا is used as a repudiation and rebuke, its meaning is like: “No way! it is not acceptable!”.
Derivative: صَعُودًا means to escalate and to keep going up. It means that the punishment will continue to get heavier and heavier.

Derivative: قُتِلَ in the past tense (الفِعْلُ المَاضِي) is a دُعاء (Du’aa – Supplication) against someone. It literally means: “May he be killed” and its meaning is like “May he be cursed or receive punishment”, preceded by the implication of: “How dare he do that!”.

قُمْ فَأَنْذِرْ

- قُمْ فَأَنْذِرْ “Rise/stand/get up, then warn”. The فَاء (Faa’ ف) separating the two commands can indicate sequence where one action is followed by another. In that case قُمْ (Rise/stand) can mean to stand in prayer, followed then by the action of أَنْذِر (Warn), after you have finished the prayer. Most of the scholars of Tafsir however link the two actions together with the meaning of: Rise up and warn i.e., initiate and embark upon the mission of warning the people.
Derivative: Is severe frowning or scowling i.e., facial expressions to show great dissatisfaction.

Derivative: يُؤْثَرُ means to leave an impact or it means to be passed down or traced back to others from أَثَر (Athar – A trace or remnant from the past) i.e., to be related from those who came before in the past.
Derivative: سَقَرَ is a noun which is Al-Mamnoo’ Min As-Sarf (diptote) and is one of the names of Jahannam (hell).
Derivative: لَوَّاحَةٌ means scorching.

Derivative: كَبِّرْ from the verb form كَبَّرَ – تَكْبِيرًا means to “continuously” declare the greatness as opposed to the form أَكْبَرَ which means to declare the greatness of something without the implication of it being continuous.

وَرَبَّكَ فَكَبِّرْ

- This verse does not only imply that the Messenger ﷺ should declare the greatness of His master. Rather, the fact that the Maf’ool Bihi (object of the verb) has been brought forward before the Fi’l (verb) implies that the declaration of greatness should be exclusively to your Rabb (master).
Derivative: أَدْبَرَ means to turn one’s back. In reference to the night, it means to draw back and recede.
Derivative: أَسْفَرَ means to completely unveil, uncover and to shine light. In reference to the morning, it refers to when the sun comes out shining its light becoming completely manifest.

إِنَّهَا لَإِحْدَى الْكُبَرِ

- “It is indeed one of the great matters” - This is the Jawaab ul-Qasm (The answer to the oath) mentioned in verse 32 كَلَّا وَالْقَمَرِ “No! (I swear) By the moon”.

Derivative: نَخُوضُ from خَاضَ – يَخُوضُ – خَوْضَا which literally means to engage, plunge or enter and immerse oneself in something. Here it refers to that has no value i.e., false talk and activities.
Derivative: الْيَقِينُ means absolute certainty and conviction. It is to refer to death as it is absolutely certain.

Derivative: الرُّجْزَ – There are a number of interpretations which have been provided for the meaning of the word الرُّجْزَ (Ar-Rujz) here. It has been said that it refers to all kinds of evil acts and statements. It has also been said to refer to the idols that were worshipped.
Derivative: مُّسْتَنفِرَةٌ means to run away and disperse quickly like animals run away when frightened.
Derivative: أَهْلُ literally means “The people of” and it is used to mean “deserving of” or “possesses the quality, competency or qualification of”.

Derivative: تَسْتَكْثِرُ from اِسْتَكْثَرَ can mean a few things in Arabic. One of the meanings is to make something a big deal or regarding it to be a great task. Another meaning is to seek to have a lot of something.

Derivative: تَمْنُنْ comes from the verb مَنَّ – يَمُنُّ. مَنَّ has multiple meanings. One of which is to give somebody a favour and then remind them or boast about it. Another meaning for مَنَّ is to be cut off or discontinued like in His statement: لَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍ “For them is a reward that is not cut off or discontinued”. Based on this second possible meaning the opinion is derived that the meaning of تَمْنُنْ is to discontinue i.e., do not discontinue your efforts and tasks that you have been given.

وَلَا تَمْنُنْ تَسْتَكْثِرُ

- By combining the meanings mentioned above for تَمْنُنْ and تَسْتَكْثِرُ the meaning of the verse can be deduced to mean: “Don’t discontinue what you have been tasked with whilst thinking that it is too much” or “Whilst you may see it as being too big a task, don’t discontinue your efforts” i.e., don’t be overwhelmed.
Derivative: اصْبِرْ is the command form of صَبَرَ – صَبْرًا. To have صَبْر (Sabr) does not only imply patience to withstand and cope with realities but also it means to persevere and continue.
Derivative: نُقِرَ from نَقَرَ means to blow accompanied by a sound that goes into the item or instrument being blown.
